headline: Tyson and JBS Stocks Drop. More Screwworm Cases Hit Texas.
summary: The U.S. Department of Agriculture confirmed three more New World screwworm cases on Monday, bringing the total to five.  The latest detections included a calf in La Salle County, Texas, a goat in Gillespie County, Texas, and a dog in Lea County, New Mexico, following two earlier cases in Zavala County, Texas.  The dog case was initially linked to Andrews County, Texas, because it was submitted by a veterinarian there.

headline: 3 Reasons to Sell TSN and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: Tyson Foods currently trades at $58.73 per share and has shown little upside over the past six months, posting a middling return of 4.5%. The stock also fell short of the S&P 500’s 10.7% gain during that period.

headline: Tyson and JBS Face a New Risk as Screwworm Hits Texas
summary: Investors in meatpackers have reasons to watch it closely.  The U.S. Department of Agriculture confirmed two more New World screwworm cases in Texas on Monday, bringing the state’s total to four.  The latest detections included a calf in La Salle County and a dog in Andrews County, following earlier cases in South Texas.

headline: Tyson Foods makes another executive change as Wes Morris takes COO role
summary: Current COO Devin Cole is retiring from the US meat giant.

headline: Goldman Sachs Adds 4 Companies to US Conviction List With Huge Double-Digit Upside Potential
summary: The Goldman Sachs Conviction List is a curated list of stocks that the firm’s research team believes are highly likely to outperform the market. It’s a tool for investors to identify stocks with strong growth potential, frequently updated to reflect changes in market conditions and company performance. headline: Tyson Foods Names Wes Morris Chief Operating Officer
summary: SPRINGDALE, Ark., June 08, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE: TSN) announced today the appointment of Wes Morris as Chief Operating Officer (COO). As COO, Morris will oversee the company’s business segments, including Chicken, Beef, Pork, Prepared Foods and International. Morris brings more than 20 years of experience with Tyson Foods, including prior leadership roles as president of the Prepared Foods and Poultry businesses.
